{"step_by_step_thinking": "Oxytocin, syntometrine, and prostaglandins are all mentioned in the documents as treatments for postpartum hemorrhage. Oxytocin is directly stated to be FDA-approved for controlling postpartum hemorrhage. Syntometrine, which is a combination of methylergonovine and oxytocin, is also listed as a treatment option. Prostaglandins, particularly misoprostol and carboprost, are described as being used for postpartum hemorrhage. However, oestrogen is not mentioned in any of the documents as a treatment for postpartum hemorrhage.", "answer_choice": "C"}
